problem_title: Continuous Sensor Transmission vs Battery Life
error: Parse error: Could not extract JSON submission from model output
raw_output:
# The 40 TRIZ Inventive Principles – Quick Reference

| # | Principle | Core Idea |
|---|-----------|----------|
| 1 | **Segmentation** | Divide object/process into independent parts; make it segmented or granular |
| 2 | **Taking Out** | Remove or separate the problematic part; extract the necessary component |
| 3 | **Local Quality** | Make different parts of an object/system have different properties |
| 4 | **Asymmetry** | Break symmetry; use asymmetric arrangements instead of symmetric ones |
| 5 | **Merging** | Combine objects, operations, or processes in time/space |
| 6 | **Universality** | Make object multifunctional; design it to perform multiple roles |
| 7 | **Nesting** | Place objects inside others; use hierarchical or telescoping structures |
| 8 | **Counterweight** | Compensate for object weight by merging with other objects |
| 9 | **Prior Action** | Perform required action in advance; pre-arrange objects before needed |
| 10 | **Preliminary Action** | Pre-arrange objects in advance to enable later beneficial action |
| 11 | **Beforehand Cushioning** | Prepare protective measures in advance; pre-compensate for harmful effects |
| 12 | **Equipotentiality** | Change conditions so object doesn't sense external forces |
| 13 | **Inversion** | Invert the action; reverse the process or move the object differently |
| 14 | **Spheroidality** | Use spherical shapes instead of flat; increase curvature or roundness |
| 15 | **Dynamics** | Make object/system adaptable or flexible; adjust to changing conditions |
| 16 | **Partial or Excessive Action** | Go beyond required in one direction; overdo one property to avoid harm |
| 17 | **Transition to a New Dimension** | Move to a higher system level; use multi-layer or 3D approaches |
| 18 | **Mechanical Vibration** | Introduce oscillation or vibration; use resonance effects |
| 19 | **Periodic Action** | Replace continuous action with periodic bursts; use pulsed operation |
| 20 | **Continuity of Useful Action** | Ensure processes continue uninterrupted; eliminate idle time |
| 21 | **Rushing Through** | Accelerate harmful processes to reduce exposure; get through damage quickly |
| 22 | **Convert Harm into Benefit** | Use harmful factors productively; make waste useful |
| 23 | **Feedback** | Introduce feedback loops; monitor and adjust automatically |
| 24 | **Intermediary** | Use intermediary objects or processes to reduce direct interaction |
| 25 | **Self-Service** | Make object serve itself; enable automatic or self-regulating operation |
| 26 | **Copying** | Use simple copies instead of complex originals; use analogs or models |
| 27 | **Cheap Short-Living Objects** | Replace expensive/durable objects with inexpensive/disposable ones |
| 28 | **Replacement of Mechanical Systems** | Replace mechanical systems with sensory, field, or smart systems |
| 29 | **Pneumatics and Hydraulics** | Use gas/fluid power instead of solid structures |
| 30 | **Flexible Shells & Thin Films** | Use flexible membranes or thin shells instead of rigid structures |
| 31 | **Porous Materials** | Make object porous; use capillary structures |
| 32 | **Color Changes** | Change color or optical properties; use optical signals |
| 33 | **Homogeneity** | Make objects of the same material; use like materials to reduce incompatibility |
| 34 | **Discarding & Recovering** | Remove parts that have completed function; recover/reuse discarded parts |
| 35 | **Parameter Changes** | Change physical properties: state, concentration, density, temperature, pressure |
| 36 | **Phase Transition** | Use state changes (solid ↔ liquid ↔ gas) to solve problems |
| 37 | **Thermal Expansion** | Use thermal expansion/contraction of materials |
| 38 | **Accelerated Oxidation** | Use oxidation or combustion processes; increase exposure to air/oxygen |
| 39 | **Inert Atmosphere** | Use inert gas or vacuum; shield from harmful external environment |
| 40 | **Composite Materials** | Use composite or laminated materials instead of homogeneous ones |

---

### **For Your IoT Sensor Problem:**

The most relevant principles for the transmission-vs.-battery contradiction are likely:
- **#19 (Periodic Action)** — transmit in bursts instead of continuous
- **#23 (Feedback)** — intelligent event-triggered transmission
- **#9 (Prior Action)** — pre-compute edge detection locally
- **#28 (Replacement of Mechanical Systems)** — use passive sensing methods
- **#35 (Parameter Changes)** — modulate transmission power dynamically

Ready to continue with matrix lookup and solution sketches?
